articles.title: ‘Do not call your defeat an agreement’ IRGC warns US, Israel
articles.summary: The spokesman for the IRGC has rejected President Trump’s suggestions that talks have been taking place with Iran.

articles.title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
articles.summary: Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC commander, will manage Iran's security amid external US-Israeli pressures and internal unrest.

articles.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
articles.summary: Iran, Israel, the Gulf faced new missiles and drones. The US has signalled military escalation and openness to a deal.

articles.title: Amid US-Israeli attacks, people in Iran struggle to survive ailing economy
articles.summary: Some are left without an income, others are temporarily displaced, and no one knows when the internet will return .

articles.title: As Iran war rocks global economy, Global South bears brunt of fallout
articles.summary: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.

articles.title: OpenAI pulls AI video app Sora as concerns grow on deepfake videos
articles.summary: This is first big step by ChatGPT maker to focus its business on potentially more lucrative areas, such as coding tools.

articles.title: Parsi: No deal ‘’without both sides giving something to the other’
articles.summary: Trita Parsi argues that Iran is unlikely to agree to end the war without sanctions relief.

articles.title: Thousands march to mark 50th anniversary of Argentina’s bloody coup
articles.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets in Buenos Aires to mark the 50th anniversary of Argentina’s last military coup.

articles.title: New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US
articles.summary: The teams are set to play on June 15 in Los Angeles, but Iran has threatened to boycott its matches due to the war.

articles.title: Indonesians fire wooden cannons in post-Eid tradition
articles.summary: Two villages in Indonesia’s West Java province faced off with wooden cannons in a traditional post-Eid celebration.

articles.title: Canada tells Israel that Lebanon’s sovereignty ‘must not be violated’
articles.summary: Canada and France call on Israel to halt plans to occupy southern Lebanon as invasion preparations under way.

articles.title: US jury orders Meta to pay $375m for endangering children
articles.summary: The verdict marks the first time a US state has successfully sued the social media giant over child safety issues.

articles.title: What we know about the US’s 15-point plan Iran proposal
articles.summary: US media reports the Trump administration has proposed a temporary ceasefire and a 15-point plan to end the war on Iran.

articles.title: US to send thousands of soldiers from 82nd Airborne Division to Middle East
articles.summary: The US is expected to deploy up to 3,000 troops from the 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East.

articles.title: Iran says ‘non-hostile’ ships can pass safely through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Tehran's statement on opening of key waterway comes as US President Trump says talks are taking place to end the war.

articles.title: Drone attack ignites fuel tank at Kuwait airport
articles.summary: A drone has sparked a fire at a fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport.

articles.title: Philippine president declares energy emergency as impact of Iran war felt
articles.summary: Transport unions say emergency declaration a 'superficial band-aid' that does not address root cause of fuel crisis.

articles.title: Philippines declares national emergency as fuel supplies dwindle
articles.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has declared a year-long national emergency as fuel supplies begin to dwindle.

articles.title: Indian police manage long lines as panic grows over fuel shortages
articles.summary: Indian police have been stationed at petrol pumps in the state of Gujarat as people panic over possible fuel shortages.

articles.title: Israeli and Palestinian mothers call for peace on barefoot walk
articles.summary: Israeli and Palestinian mothers marched barefoot through Rome, calling for greater protection for children.

articles.title: Iran war live: Iranian military dismisses Trump’s claim of negotiations
articles.summary: US-Israeli strikes on Iran continue despite claims of talks, with at least 12 killed, 28 wounded in an attack in Tehran.

articles.title: Russian weapons, tactics seen in Ukraine are shaping Myanmar’s civil war
articles.summary: Russian role felt in Myanmar, from aircraft to waves of conscripts launching 'meat assaults' made infamous in Ukraine.

articles.title: Activists label Larry Ellison’s yacht ‘The Trump Propagandist’
articles.summary: Led By Donkeys released a video showing members labelling Larry Ellison’s superyacht “The Trump Propagandist.”

articles.title: Trump keeps up claims of talks with ‘the right people’ in Iran
articles.summary: US reportedly engaged in backchannel efforts, though Israel apparently not on same page, and military buildup continues.

articles.title: Explosion, fire at Erbil high-rise amid Iranian attacks
articles.summary: Video shows a large explosion and fire at a high-rise building in Erbil reportedly near the US Consulate.
